7: This code creates a search-augmented agent. When this agent receives a question, it
8: will not just rely on its pre-existing knowledge. Instead, following its instructions, it will
9: use the Google Search tool to find relevant, real-time information from the web and then
10: use that information to construct its answer.
12: CrewAI offers an orchestration framework for building multi-agent systems by focusing
13: on collaborative roles and structured processes. It operates at a higher level of
14: abstraction than foundational toolkits, providing a conceptual model that mirrors a
15: human team. Instead of defining the granular flow of logic as a graph, the developer
16: defines the actors and their assignments, and CrewAI manages their interaction.
17: The core components of this framework are Agents, Tasks, and the Crew. An Agent is
18: defined not just by its function but by a persona, including a specific role, a goal, and a
19: backstory, which guides its behavior and communication style. A Task is a discrete unit
20: of work with a clear description and expected output, assigned to a specific Agent. The
21: Crew is the cohesive unit that contains the Agents and the list of Tasks, and it executes
22: a predefined Process. This process dictates the workflow, which is typically either
23: sequential, where the output of one task becomes the input for the next in line, or
24: hierarchical, where a manager-like agent delegates tasks and coordinates the workflow
26: When compared to other frameworks, CrewAI occupies a distinct position. It moves
27: away from the low-level, explicit state management and control flow of LangGraph,
28: where a developer wires together every node and conditional edge. Instead of building
29: a state machine, the developer designs a team charter. While Googlés ADK provides a
30: comprehensive, production-oriented platform for the entire agent lifecycle, CrewAI
31: concentrates specifically on the logic of agent collaboration and for simulating a team of
